title: Unexpected error in prepared statement
description:
### What happens?

Hi,

The following prepared statement triggers an error `Cannot mix values of type BOOLEAN and FLOAT in BETWEEN clause - an explicit cast is required`:

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 REAL, PRIMARY KEY(c0));
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);
PREPARE prepare_query AS SELECT (? NOT BETWEEN ((NOT t0.c0) ::BOOL) AND t0.c0) FROM t0;
```

However, the equivalent normal select query can execute successfully:

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 REAL, PRIMARY KEY(c0));
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);
SELECT (2 NOT BETWEEN ((NOT t0.c0) ::BOOL) AND t0.c0) FROM t0;
```

### To Reproduce

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 REAL, PRIMARY KEY(c0));
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);
PREPARE prepare_query AS SELECT (? NOT BETWEEN ((NOT t0.c0) ::BOOL) AND t0.c0) FROM t0;
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 24.04

### DuckDB Version:

v1.4.0-dev2263

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

poc: CREATE TABLE t0(c0 REAL, PRIMARY KEY(c0));
SELECT (? NOT BETWEEN ((NOT t0.c0) ::BOOL) AND t0.c0) FROM t0;

SELECT (CAST(? AS REAL) NOT BETWEEN ((NOT t0.c0)::BOOL)::REAL AND t0.c0) 
FROM t0;
key_bug_features: Prepared statement with BOOLEAN and FLOAT in BETWEEN clause
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c1 BOOLEAN, c2 FLOAT, PRIMARY KEY(c1, c2));
PREPARE prepare_query_1 AS SELECT (? NOT BETWEEN ((NOT t1.c1) ::BOOL) AND t1.c2) FROM t1;
SELECT (CAST(? AS FLOAT) NOT BETWEEN ((NOT t1.c1)::BOOL)::FLOAT AND t1.c2) FROM t1;
CREATE TABLE t2(c3 INT, c4 REAL DEFAULT 0.0, PRIMARY KEY(c3));
